channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Christopher Dontell pleads guilty to charges in 2020 killing of Gregory Rice
item.description: Rice's body was found in the Pee Dee River on Nov. 8, 2020, according to authorities who said he had been shot multiple times.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Bright, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects a former soldier and Iraq War veteran to serve as Army secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Wednesday that he has selected a former soldier and Iraq War veteran to serve as secretary of the Army. Trump said Daniel P. Driscoll had completed Army Ranger school and deployed with the 10th Mountain Division to Iraq.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecutors: DC police officer’s talk with Proud Boys leader grew secretive as arrest neared
item.description: Metropolitan Police Department Lt. Shane Lamond’s communication with the former leader of the Proud Boys increased and grew more secretive as pressure to arrest him mounted in 2020, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump considers DeSantis for the Pentagon with Hegseth under pressure over allegations: AP sources
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, Donald Trump's Pentagon pick, was fighting to hold on to his Cabinet nomination amid growing questions Wednesday about his personal conduct as the president-elect's team considers alternatives, including Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, ZEKE MILLER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida man intentionally drove truck into USAA building: Police
item.description: Robert Beaty, 70, was denied entry to the building when he arrived at around 2:12 p.m. and demanded to speak to a USAA representative.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO is killed in a 'targeted shooting' outside a New York hotel, officials say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was shot and killed Wednesday morning in what police said was a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding an investor conference. The 50-year-old executive was shot around 6:45 a.m.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KAREN MATTHEWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: Wisconsin’s upcoming Supreme Court election already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court Latest: Majority of justices appear likely to uphold state ban on transgender care
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments on Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California farmers are hopeful Trump administration will deliver more water to fields
item.description: Since winning the election, President-elect Donald Trump has been talking about immigration, border security and government efficiency. But in California farm country, his comments about water are also getting top attention.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Majority of Supreme Court seems reluctant to block state bans on medical treatments for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, a majority of the Supreme Court seemed reluctant Wednesday to block Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aurora apartment caretaker says residents are not paying rent
item.description: The apartments were associated with The Edge at Lowry apartments, where a viral video was taken of men in a stairwell.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's family says search is 'far from over' after police reveal she voluntarily left for Mexico
item.description: Authorities may consider Hannah Kobayashi a "voluntary missing person," but her family isn't done looking for her.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KRON4

item.title: Trump team outlines inauguration benefits for donors
item.description: The team highlighted that committee members who give $1 million or raise $2 million receive six tickets to access exclusive inaugural events between Saturday, Jan. 18, and Monday, Jan. 20, the day of the swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Former Abercrombie CEO's partner pleads not guilty to sex trafficking charges, is freed on bond
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — Matthew Smith, the partner of former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, was released on $10 million bond Tuesday in their sex trafficking case with conditions including home detention and GPS monitoring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: We're all saying Daniel Craig's name wrong
item.description: His name may no longer be "Bond, James Bond," but former 007 Daniel Craig is setting the record straight about how to pronounce his name.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KRON4

item.title: The drinking water for New Jersey's capital went largely untested for more than a year, utility says
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — The drinking water in New Jersey's capital city and some surrounding communities went largely untested for more than a year, the utility belatedly told roughly 200,000 affected customers, noting that it fired a worker who falsified the relevant reports.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GM recalls over 130,000 trucks over unexpected tailgate unlatching
item.description: Over 132,000 Chevrolet and GMC trucks are facing a recall over concerns that the tailgate may unlatch unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's the top 25 list of most-viewed Wikipedia articles of 2024
item.description: We collectively spent an estimated 2.4 billion hours reading English-language articles on Wikipedia in 2024, and we now know which were the most popular.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, KRON4

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: LAPD give update on Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles
item.description: Authorities are set to update the public Monday evening with the latest details on Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old Maui resident who has been the center of a massive search since she vanished in Los Angeles in early November.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Law enforcement leaders react to Trump selecting Hillsborough Sheriff Chad Chronister as DEA Administrator
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday afternoon he's nominating Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister to be the next Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA).
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service said Monday nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week for this year's presidential contest, despite hurricanes, some misdirected election mail and delivery concerns raised by state officials.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS warns holiday shoppers to be cautious of scams
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service and its Security Summit partners issued a warning Monday, urging taxpayers to exercise extra caution during holiday shopping to avoid falling victim to identity theft scams.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions scored a major legal victory Monday with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law that sparked weeks of protests and made the state the center of the national battle over union rights.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutor says officers acted 'recklessly' in Black man's death during mental health crisis
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers charged in the death of a Black man — who was shocked with a Taser during a mental health crisis — acted “recklessly" by restraining him face down longer than necessary, a prosecutor said during opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Intel CEO steps down as chipmaker falters, lays off staff
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stepped down after a three-year stint leading the company, the chipmaker announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio schools offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: MARYSVILLE, Ohio (WCMH) -- A new religious release program for public school students is entering Marysville schools, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Best Cyber Monday 2023 tablet deals that make great gifts
item.description: BestReviews is reader-supported and may earn an affiliate commission. Details. Score deals on tablets from Apple, Samsung and Amazon Cyber Monday is here, and one constant is that you can find major savings on tech like tablets, both online and in stores.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Luna, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Philadelphia defense, Tucker's misses help the Eagles to a 24-19 win in their showdown with Ravens
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Saquon Barkley vs. Derrick Henry was never supposed to be a head-to-head matchup. “It ain't basketball. It ain't like I go out there and tackle Derrick Henry,” Barkley said. “To be honest, I don't know if I really want to tackle Derrick Henry.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5
